blocks:
Ayahs

حمٓ (۱)
Ha, Meem. (1)
تَنزِيلٌ مِّنَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ (۲)
A [gradually] sent down [revelation] from the All-beneficent, the All-merciful, (2)
كِتَـٰبٌ فُصِّلَتْ ءَايَـٰتُهُۥ قُرْءَانًا عَرَبِيًّا لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ (۳)
[this is] a Book whose signs have been elaborated for a people who have knowledge, an Arabic Quran, (3)
بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا فَأَعْرَضَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُونَ (٤)
a bearer of good news and a warner. But most of them turn away [from it], [and] so they do not listen. (4)

Key Points

The word «anazalnā» means instantaneous descent (nuzūl daf‘ī), and the word «tanzīl» means gradual descent (nuzūl tadrījī). Perhaps, to reconcile the two expressions «anazalnā» and «tanzīl», it can be said that the concepts and content of the Quran were revealed all at once upon the blessed heart of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله on the Night of Qadr (Laylat al-Qadr), but the words and forms were revealed gradually.(2) Wherever the descent (nuzūl) of the Quran is mentioned, there is a sign of nurturing, decisiveness, might, wisdom, and mercy involved. «Tanzīlun min Rabbil-‘Ālamīn» (3) «Tanzīlul-Kitābi lā rayba fīh» (4) «Tanzīlun minallāhil-‘Azīzil-Ḥakīm» (5) «Tanzīlun minar-Raḥmānir-Raḥīm» (6) «Tanzīlun min Ḥakīmin Ḥamīd» (7)

(2) Tafsir Rahnama. 3) Al-Waqi'ah, 80 and Al-Haqqah, 43. 4) As-Sajdah, 2. 5) Al-Jathiyah, 2 and Al-Ahqaf, 2. 6) Fussilat, 2. 7) Fussilat, 42.

Messages

1- The Quran was revealed gradually in various contexts. «Tanzīl»

2- The Quran, its gradual descent, and the purpose of its descent, arise from His vast and eternal Mercy. «Tanzīlun minar-Raḥmānir-Raḥīm»

3- The Quran existed as a book during the time of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. «Kitāb»

4- Glorifying a person or a book is sometimes necessary. («Fuṣṣilat», «Āyātuh», «‘Arabiyyan», «Nadhīran», «Bashīran» are attributes of the Quran.)

5- The Quran speaks in detail, decisively, clearly, and without ambiguity about everything effective in guiding and developing people (such as commands and prohibitions, stories and lessons, arguments, parables, descriptions of blessings, the future of humanity, the events of the Day of Judgment, the factors of might and downfall, etc.). «Fuṣṣilat Āyātuh»

6- Not only the content but also the words (lafẓ) of the Quran are from Allah. «Tanzīl... Qur’ānan ‘Arabiyyan»

7- The detail and eloquence of the Quran are understood by those who are familiar with the Arabic language. «Qur’ānan ‘Arabiyyan liqawmin ya‘lamūn» (Perhaps the intent of «ya‘lamūn» is knowledge of the Arabic language).

8- The Quran was revealed for all people «Hudan linnās» (8), but only the people of knowledge and piety benefit from it. «Liqawmin ya‘lamūn – Hudan lil-Muttaqīn»

9- Knowledge is not merely reading and writing, but understanding the truth. «Liqawmin ya‘lamūn» (Considering that many of those guided in early Islam did not have the ability to read and write).

10- Fear and hope are necessary side by side. «Bashīran wa Nadhīran»

11- Majority is not proof of righteousness. «Fa-a‘raḍa aktharuhum»

12- The reason people turn away from the Quran is their ignorance. «Liqawmin ya‘lamūn... fa-a‘raḍa aktharuhum» (8) Al-An‘am, 91.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Fussilat

This Surah is Makki and has fifty-four verses. The name of this Surah is derived from the third verse, and it is also called «Ḥā Mīm as-Sajdah» because it begins with «Ḥā Mīm» and is the first among the four Surahs that contain an obligatory prostration (sajdah wājibah). The verses of this Surah concern the Resurrection, the history of previous nations, the greatness of the Quran, and the signs of Allah’s power in existence. We read in a tradition that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله used to recite Surah Tabārak and Ḥā Mīm as-Sajdah every night before sleeping.(1)

(1) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani.

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ قُلُوبُنَا فِىٓ أَكِنَّةٍ مِّمَّا تَدْعُونَآ إِلَيْهِ وَفِىٓ ءَاذَانِنَا وَقْرٌ وَمِنۢ بَيْنِنَا وَبَيْنِكَ حِجَابٌ فَٱعْمَلْ إِنَّنَا عَـٰمِلُونَ (٥)
They say, ‘Our hearts are in veils [which shut them off] from what you invite us to, and there is a deafness in our ears, and there is a curtain between us and you. So act [as your faith requires]; we too are acting [according to our own].’ (5)

Key Points

"Akinna" (أكنّة) is the plural of "kinān" (كِنان), meaning a cloth in which something is wrapped. (9) "Waqr" (وقر) means heaviness in the ear (deafness). The disbelievers adopted five severe stances against the Prophet and the Quran: A) Turning away and distancing themselves. "Fa a'raḍa aktharuhum" (فاعرض اكثرهم) (in the previous Ayah) B) Lack of readiness in the heart to accept the truth. "Qulūbunā fī akinnah" (قلوبنا فى اكنة) C) Not listening to the messages. "Fī ādhāninā waqr" (فى آذاننا وقر) D) Declaring the existence of a barrier. (Meaning the veil of self-conceit, worldliness, etc.) "Baynanā wa baynaka ḥijāb" (بیننا و بینك حجاب) E) Insisting on their own path. "Fa'mal innanā 'āmilūn" (فاعمل اننا عاملون)

9) Mufradāt al-Rāghib.

Messages

1- The work of Allah is to bestow grace and send down revelation from the source of Mercy. "Tanzīlun min al-Raḥmān al-Raḥīm" (تنزیل من الرحمن الرحیم). But the method of the disbelievers is obstinacy and turning away. "Qulūbunā fī akinnah" (قلوبنا فى اكنة).

2- If the heart is under one covering, it is capable of being warned, but multiple coverings make the task impossible. ("Akinna" (أكنّة) is used in the plural form).

3- If the audience is not ready, divine revelation, Allah's Mercy, the heavenly Book, and being a bearer of good tidings (bashīr) and a warner (nadhīr) will be of no benefit. (Considering the previous Ayah and this Ayah).

4- The first condition for accepting the truth is hearing the word of truth, which the opponents deprive themselves of. "Fī ādhāninā waqr" (فى اذاننا وقر).

5- "Moses to his religion, Jesus to his religion" (a common saying implying separation/indifference) is the speech of those whose hearts are veiled by the barrier of fanaticism and obstinacy. "Min bayninā wa baynaka ḥijāb fa'mal innanā 'āmilūn" (من بیننا و بینك حجاب فاعمل اننّا عاملون).

Ayahs

قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ أَنَّمَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ فَٱسْتَقِيمُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوهُ ۗ وَوَيْلٌ لِّلْمُشْرِكِينَ (٦)
Say, ‘I am just a human like you. It has been revealed to me that your God is the One God. So worship Him single-mindedly and plead to Him for forgiveness.’ And woe to the polytheists (6)
ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْتُونَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَهُم بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۷)
—those who do not pay the zakat and disbelieve in the Hereafter. (7)

Key Points

Considering that the ruling regarding Zakat (obligatory charity) was revealed in the second year of the Hijra in Medina, and this Surah is Meccan, the intent of Zakat in this Ayah is financial aid. We read in the narrations that the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله swore that not paying Zakat is treachery and polytheism (Shirk) (10). And whoever does not pay Zakat, even the amount of a Qirat (a small measure), is neither a complete believer (Mu'min Kamil) nor a complete Muslim, nor does he possess dignity. And whoever does not pay Zakat dies either a Jew or a Christian (11). In the culture of the Quran, indifference to some of the Divine commands is considered tantamount to disbelief (Kufr) and polytheism (Shirk). Regarding abandoning Hajj, we read: «و للّه على الناس حج البیت من استطاع الیه سبیلا و من كفر...» (12) (Hajj is) obligatory upon everyone who is able to go to Hajj, and whoever disbelieves... It is stated in Al-Kafi: قلت فمن لم یحج منا فقد كفر فقال: لا، لكن من قال: لیس هذا هكذا فقد كفر.(13) According to this narration, abandoning Hajj is not disbelief, but denying the obligation of Hajj is disbelief. Regarding abandoning prayer (Salat), the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: «ما بین المسلم و بین الكافر الاّ ان یترك الصلوة الفریضة متعمداً» (14) (A Muslim who intentionally abandons the obligatory prayer is like a disbeliever.) And in this Ayah, regarding abandoning Zakat, we read: «ویل للمشركین الّذین لا یؤتون الزكوة» (Abandoning Zakat is like polytheism/Shirk). Zakat is the budget of the Islamic government, and not paying it means not recognizing the monotheistic system, which is equivalent to polytheism (Shirk).

10) Bihar, Vol. 96, p. 29. 11) Mizan al-Hikmah, entry: Zakat. 12) Al Imran, 97. 13) Tafsir Burhan. 14) Bihar, Vol. 77, p. 58.

Messages

  1. Do not retreat from the goal in the face of the harshest obstinacy. (The polytheists demanded the execution of the threats: «فاعمل فاننا عاملون» Allah commands [the Prophet] to tell them that "I am not responsible for punishment, retribution, or carrying out threats; I am only responsible for conveying the revelation." «قل انما انا بشر...»)

  2. Monotheism (Tawhid) is the headline of the Prophets' call. «انما الهكم اله واحد»

  3. Allah is Unique; be steadfast in His path. «الهكم الهٌ واحد فاستقیموا الیه»

  4. Compensate for the future by being steadfast in the path of Monotheism, and for the past by seeking forgiveness and apology from the Divine Presence. «فاستقیموا الیه و استغفروه»

  5. Not paying Zakat and indifference toward the poor is situated between polytheism (Shirk) and disbelief (Kufr) and is highly dangerous. «ویل للمشركین الّذین لا یؤتون الزكوة و هم بالاخرة هم كافرون»

  6. Disbelief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) leads to not paying Zakat. «لا یؤتون الزكوة و هم بالاخرة هم كافرون»

  7. The source of not paying Zakat is either worldliness, which is a type of polytheism (Shirk), or not believing in the Day of Judgment, which is disbelief (Kufr). «مشركین - كافرون»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ (۸)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, there will be an everlasting reward for them. (8)
۞ قُلْ أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَكْفُرُونَ بِٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلْأَرْضَ فِى يَوْمَيْنِ وَتَجْعَلُونَ لَهُۥٓ أَندَادًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ رَبُّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۹)
Say, ‘Do you really disbelieve in Him who created the earth in two days, and ascribe partners to Him? That is the Lord of all the worlds!’ (9)

Key Points

Mamnoon (ممنون) means cut off or subject to favor/obligation (minnah). In Ayah 5, we read that the disbelievers expressed their obstinacy using five different phrases, saying: "Our hearts are covered," and "Our ears are heavy," and so on. In this Ayah, Allah tells His Prophet: Do not pay attention to their hard-hearted statements, and continue your work through questioning and argumentation. "Say: Is it that you disbelieve..." (قل ءَانكم لتكفرون)

The intent behind the creation of the Earth in two days is two stages or periods. Before the creation of the earth and the heavens and the emergence of night and day, month and year, the concept of "day" did not exist for us to say that Allah created the Earth in two days.

Allah's power is exercised alongside His wisdom. Although Allah could create the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them with a single command, and He has spoken of this power many times using the phrase Kun fa-yakūn ("Be, and it is"), He placed the creation of the heavens and the earth in several stages and periods. This indicates that the exercise of power must be accompanied by expediency (maslahah), and the expediency of creation lay in multiple stages.

Question: In seven instances, the Quran states that creation took place in six days: four instances concern the creation of the earth and the heavens (15), and three instances concern the creation of the earth, the heavens, and what is between them (16). Of course, from the latter three Ayahs, it can be understood that the first Ayahs also relate to the creation of the heavens, the earth, and what is between them. A question arises here: In this Ayah, it states that the earth was created in two days, and in Ayah 12, it states that the heavens were also created in two days. In Ayah 10, it states that your provision was decreed in four days, which totals eight days, contradicting the Ayahs mentioning six days.

Answer: Creation is different from the decreeing of provision (taqdir al-rizq). Therefore, combining these two to make eight days is incorrect. Rather, these Ayahs only refer to four days out of the total six days of creation, and the other two days relate to the creation of what is between the heavens and the earth, or other matters about which these Ayahs are silent.

(15) Al-A'raf, 54; Yunus, 3; Hud, 7; and Al-Hadid, 4. (16) Al-Furqan, 59; As-Sajdah, 4; and Qaf, 38.

Messages

1- Alongside the threat against the disbelievers, praise for the righteous is necessary. "Woe to the polytheists... Indeed, those who have believed..." (ویلٌ للمشركین... ان الّذین آمنوا...)

2- Faith and righteous deeds are inseparable. "They have believed and done righteous deeds." (آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات)

3- Faith precedes action. (Āmanū [believed] comes before ‘amilū [acted]).

4- Those who perform deeds without expecting favor (minnah) in this world will receive a reward without obligation (minnah) in Paradise. "For them is a reward uninterrupted." (لهم اجر غیر ممنون)

5- The Great God does not impose obligation (minnah) for all those eternal blessings in Paradise. (How ugly it is for us to impose obligation for a small, temporary favor). "A reward uninterrupted." (اجر غیر ممنون)

6- The best way to invite the disbelievers is by stating Allah's grace. "Is it that you disbelieve in the One who created..." (لتكفرون بالّذى خلق...)

7- The creation of the Earth occurred gradually and over two periods. "He created the earth in two days." (خلق الارض فى یومین)

8- How can you ascribe partners to Allah, when He had no partner in the creation of the Earth? "He created the earth in two days, and you attribute equals to Him." (خلق الارض فى یومین و تجعلون له انداداً)

9- The Creator is the One and Only Lord of the Worlds. "He created... the Lord of the Worlds." (خَلق... ربّ العالمین)

Ayahs

وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا رَوَٰسِىَ مِن فَوْقِهَا وَبَـٰرَكَ فِيهَا وَقَدَّرَ فِيهَآ أَقْوَٰتَهَا فِىٓ أَرْبَعَةِ أَيَّامٍ سَوَآءً لِّلسَّآئِلِينَ (۱٠)
He set in it firm mountains, [rising] above it, blessed it, and ordained in it, in four days, [various] means of sustenance, equally for all seekers. (10)
ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰٓ إِلَى ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَهِىَ دُخَانٌ فَقَالَ لَهَا وَلِلْأَرْضِ ٱئْتِيَا طَوْعًا أَوْ كَرْهًا قَالَتَآ أَتَيْنَا طَآئِعِينَ (۱۱)
Then He turned to the heaven, and it was smoke, and He said to it and to the earth, ‘Come, willingly or unwillingly!’ They said, ‘We come obediently.’ (11)

Key Points

«رواسى» (Rawāsi) is the plural of «راسیة» (Rāsiyah), meaning firm mountains. «سواء» (Sawā') means equal, and «سواء للسائلین» (Sawā' li-s-sā'ilīn) means that the provisions are equal to the needs. «طَوع» (Ṭaw‘) means willingness, and «كَره» (Karh) means unwillingness. The phrase «استوى» (Istawa) when accompanied by the preposition «على» (‘alā), means dominance and sovereignty, like the Ayah «الرّحمن على العرش استوى» (17). But when accompanied by the preposition «الى» (ilā), it means attention and intention, like this very Ayah (18).

Creation has stages of development: 1) Being created. «خلق الارض» 2) Establishment and stabilization. «جعل فیها رواسى» 3) Injecting benefits into the earth. «بارك فیها» 4) Providing for the needs of all. «سواء للسائلین»

The earth and mountains are sources of blessing. Some of the benefits and blessings of the earth include: providing foodstuffs, the emergence of plants, water purification, burying carcasses and waste, taking one seed and yielding several sheaves, absorbing waste and giving clear water. And in the mountains, there are also blessings such as various types of minerals, preserving snow, providing building stones, preventing earth tremors, controlling storms and winds, serving as landmarks and guides for travelers, etc.

Some have taken «اربعة ایّام» (four days) to refer to the four seasons and have said that the change of seasons is effective in providing sustenance for the creatures of the earth.

Question: If sustenance is for everyone, why are there so many hungry people? Answer: In Surah Ibrahim, Ayahs 32 to 34, Allah says: We sent down rain and grew fruits to be your provision. We subjected ships, rivers, the sun, the moon, and the night and day to you, and We gave you everything you asked for. If you try to count the blessings of Allah, you cannot enumerate them. However, deficient management, unjust distribution, extravagance, wastefulness, and oppressive actions have brought these miseries upon you. And at the end, He says: «ان الانسان لظلوم كفّار» (Indeed, man is unjust and ungrateful).

17) Taha, 5. 18) Mufradāt Rāghib.

Messages

1- The emergence of mountains is not a random event, but was planned. «جعل فیها رواسى»

2- The decreeing of sustenance and the placement of blessing in the earth are among the affairs of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّ العالمین - بارك فیها - قدّر فیها»

3- Allah has decreed sustenance for all creatures living on earth, based on wisdom and in the necessary amount. «قدّر فیها اقواتها»

4- The decreeing of provisions occurred before our creation. «قدّر فیها اقواتها فى اربعة ایّام»

We did not exist, nor did we make a request, Your grace hears what we have not spoken.

5- All earthly creatures have an equal and uniform right to the provision of their needs. «سواء للسائلین»

6- To attain sustenance, one must strive. (The word «للسائلین» (li-s-sā'ilīn) is a sign of asking, and true asking is accompanied by effort and endeavor).

7- The heavens were initially in the form of smoke and gas. «و هى دخان»

8- We read a few Ayahs earlier that the disbelievers used to tell the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله: Our hearts are in coverings of veils and our ears are heavy. But this Ayah states that the heavens and the earth hear the command of Allah and obey submissively. «اتینا طائعین»

9- Existence possesses consciousness and is addressed. «اتینا طائعین»

Ayah

فَقَضَىٰهُنَّ سَبْعَ سَمَـٰوَاتٍ فِى يَوْمَيْنِ وَأَوْحَىٰ فِى كُلِّ سَمَآءٍ أَمْرَهَا ۚ وَزَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِمَصَـٰبِيحَ وَحِفْظًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْعَلِيمِ (۱۲)
Then He set them up as seven heavens in two days, and revealed [to the angels] in each heaven its ordinance. We have adorned the lowest heaven with lamps, and guarded them. That is the ordaining of the All-mighty, the All-knowing. (12)

Key Points

Although Allah could have created everything with a single command (or will), His wisdom necessitated that He create the heavens in two stages and periods. In this Ayah, the discussion of the stars is presented under several titles: Adornment of the heaven. (zīnāh) Protection of the heaven. (ḥifāẓan) Lamps of the heaven. (maṣābīḥ)

In Sūrat al-Ṣāffāt, we read: «انّا زیّنا السماء الدنیا بزینة الكواكب و حفظاً من كل شیطان مارد لایسمعون الى الملاء الأعلى و یقذفون من كل جانب دحورا و لهم عذاب واصب»(19) We adorned the nearest (lower) heaven with the adornment of the stars so that We might protect it from every rebellious devil. They cannot listen to (the speech of) the angels of the higher realm, for they are targeted from every side and driven back, and for them is a perpetual punishment.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: My Ahl al-Bayt (Household) are a means of security for the inhabitants of the earth, just as the stars are a source of security for the heavens.(20)

19) Saffat, 6 - 9. 20) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq.

Messages

1- Allah has made the heaven seven layers (or heavens). «فقضاهنّ سبع سموات»

2- In the sight of Allah's power, the creation of the earth does not differ from the creation of the seven heavens. He created both in two stages. «فقضاهنّ... فى یومین»

3- Although every heaven has a specific command and characteristic, all are connected to a single source. «اوحى...»

4- The programs (or affairs) of each heaven are separate from the others. (amrahā means the affairs related to that specific heaven.)

5- Everything we have seen of the stars, and whatever will be discovered later, are adornments of the lowest heaven, and we have no knowledge of the other heavens. «زیّنا السماء الدنیا بمصابیح»

6- The stars are a factor in the protection of the heavens. «حفظاً»

7- Divine decrees are not accessible to anyone. «تقدیر العزیز»

8- Existence is the manifestation of Divine knowledge and power. «ذلك تقدیر العزیز العلیم»

9- Divine decrees are wise and just. «ذلك تقدیر العزیز العلیم»

Ayahs

فَإِنْ أَعْرَضُوا۟ فَقُلْ أَنذَرْتُكُمْ صَـٰعِقَةً مِّثْلَ صَـٰعِقَةِ عَادٍ وَثَمُودَ (۱۳)
But if they turn away, say, ‘I warn you of a thunderbolt, like the thunderbolt of ‘Ad and Thamud.’ (13)
إِذْ جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلرُّسُلُ مِنۢ بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ ۖ قَالُوا۟ لَوْ شَآءَ رَبُّنَا لَأَنزَلَ مَلَـٰٓئِكَةً فَإِنَّا بِمَآ أُرْسِلْتُم بِهِۦ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۱٤)
When the apostles came to them, before them and in their own time, saying, ‘Worship no one except Allah!’ They said, ‘Had our Lord wished, He would have sent down angels [to us]. We indeed disbelieve in what you have been sent with.’ (14)

Key Points

One of the excuses of the disbelievers (kuffār) and polytheists (mushrikūn) against the prophets was their saying: If Allah had wished to guide us, He would have sent one of the angels as a prophet for us, not that He should appoint a human being like ourselves as our prophet. Whereas, the prophet must be of our own kind so that he can be a role model for our lives and understand our needs.

Messages

1- Divine leaders must anticipate the deviation of the people and think about its remedy. «فان اعرضوا»

2- Warning (indhār) is necessary for reforming the arrogant. «انذرتكم»

3- Every glad tiding and warning the prophets delivered was from Allah. «فقل انذرتكم»

4- Not all punishments occur in the Hereafter. «مثل صاعقة عاد...»

5- History possesses fixed traditions (Sunan). «صاعقة مثل صاعقة عاد...»

6- Divine wrath follows the completion of the proof (itmām al-ḥujjah). «اذ جاءتهم الرسل»

7- Prophets and religious preachers must have an active presence in society and strive from every direction. «اذ جاءتهم الرسل من بین ایدیهم و من خلقهم»

8- The successive arrival of prophets is a sign of Allah's concern for guiding the people. «من بین ایدیهم...»

9- The foremost duty of the prophets is the call to Monotheism (Tawḥīd). «الا تعبدوا الاّ اللّه»

10- Every arrogant person fabricates an excuse and justification for his deviation. «لو شاء ربّنا...»

11- Opposition to the prophets was staunch. (Various forms of emphasis are used in the phrase «انا بما ارسلتم به كافرون»).

12- The opposition of the disbelievers (kuffār) is against the Divine doctrine, not against the person of the Prophet. «بما ارسلتم به كافرون»

Ayah

فَأَمَّا عَادٌ فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ وَقَالُوا۟ مَنْ أَشَدُّ مِنَّا قُوَّةً ۖ أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَهُمْ هُوَ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً ۖ وَكَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يَجْحَدُونَ (۱٥)
As for [the people of] ‘Ad, they acted arrogantly in the earth unduly, and they said, ‘Who is more powerful than us?’ Did they not see that Allah, who created them, is more powerful than them? They used to impugn Our signs; (15)

Key Points

The people of 'Ād, who lived in the south of the Arabian Peninsula, were strong and warlike individuals. They possessed sturdy fortresses and tall buildings, and these things led to their arrogance and pride. (21) Power and strength are valuable only if we recognize them as coming from Allah and desire them for divine goals. Prophet Lot (عليه السلام), in order to restrain the people from corruption, wished for strength from Allah. «لو انّ لى بكم قوّة» (22) Islam advises people to prepare every kind of strength possible to intimidate the enemies of Allah. «و اعدّوا لهم ما استطعتم من قوّة» (23) However, if power becomes a means of arrogance and pride, we will suffer the fate of 'Ād.

21) Tafsir Namūneh. 22) Hūd, 80. 23) Al-Anfāl, 60.

Messages

1- In recounting history, we should only focus on what serves as a lesson. (The Quran does not mention the number of the people of 'Ād, their city, or their tribe). «فامّا عاد...»

2- Arrogance leads to destruction. «صاعقة عاد... فاستكبروا»

3- The pride of a weak human being before the All-Dominant Allah is completely misplaced. «استكبروا... بغیر الحقّ»

4- The motivation for pride is being deluded by power and having no rival. «من اشدّ منّا قوّة»

5- The remedy for combating self-superiority is paying attention to Divine power. «من اشدّ منّا قوّة... الّذى خلقهم هو اشدّ منهم»

6- Power is not a sign of righteousness (truth). «من اشدّ منّا قوّة...»

7- A look at one's own creation, the weaknesses during the embryonic and childhood stages, and the needs of today removes arrogance. «الّذى خلقهم هو اشد منهم»

8- What leads the disbelievers to destruction is persistence in disbelief and obstinacy. «كانوا - یجحدون»

Ayah

فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحًا صَرْصَرًا فِىٓ أَيَّامٍ نَّحِسَاتٍ لِّنُذِيقَهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْخِزْىِ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَخْزَىٰ ۖ وَهُمْ لَا يُنصَرُونَ (۱٦)
so We unleashed upon them an icy gale during ill-fated days, in order that We might make them taste a humiliating punishment in the life of the world. Yet the punishment of the Hereafter will be surely more disgraceful, and they will not be helped. (16)

Key Points

In Surah Al-Haqqah, starting from Ayah 6, we read the following about the people of 'Ad: The people of 'Ad were destroyed by a cold, poisonous wind that blew upon them for seven nights and eight days, and they fell to the ground like the trunks of date palms.

Messages

1- Natural factors are agents executing the command of Allah. «فارسلنا علیهم ریحاً»

2- The cause of divine punishment is the actions of the people themselves. «كانوا بآیاتنا یجحدون - فارسلنا»

3- By the will of Allah, the constructive role of the wind is transformed into a destructive (burning) role. «ریحاً صرصرا»

4- Divine wrath sometimes destroys a people in an instant, and sometimes gradually over several days and nights. «فى ایّامٍ»

5- Good and bad events that occur during the day or night divide those days and nights into blessed (mubarak) and ominous (nahs). «ایّام نحساتٍ»

6- The bitterness of this world is merely a taste compared to the Hereafter. «لنذیقهم»

7- The consequence of arrogance (istikbar) is humiliation. «فاستكبروا... عذاب الخزى»

8- Those who humiliated the prophets with the slogan «انّا بما ارسلتم به كافرون»(24) will be humiliated in this world and the Hereafter. «الخزى - اخزى » (24) Fussilat, 14.

Ayahs

وَأَمَّا ثَمُودُ فَهَدَيْنَـٰهُمْ فَٱسْتَحَبُّوا۟ ٱلْعَمَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ فَأَخَذَتْهُمْ صَـٰعِقَةُ ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْهُونِ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (۱۷)
As for [the people of] Thamud, We guided them, but they preferred blindness to guidance. So the bolt of a humiliating punishment seized them because of what they used to earn. (17)
وَنَجَّيْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ يَتَّقُونَ (۱۸)
And We delivered those who had faith and were Godwary. (18)

Key Points

The Prophet of the people of Thamud was Prophet Salih. They lived between Medina and Sham (Wadi al-Qura) and possessed high agricultural capabilities and physical strength. Some verses describe the destruction of the people of Thamud by a thunderbolt (or lightning), and others by an earthquake, which could have occurred simultaneously. «صاعقة العذاب»

Messages

1- History is the best lesson to learn from. «و امّا ثمود...»

2- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to guide people. «فهدیناهم»

3- The disbelief (Kufr) of some people is immediate, without contemplation or reason. «فاستحبّوا...» (The letter fa [ف] indicates haste in disbelief.)

4- Man is free and possesses the power of choice. «فاستحبّوا العمى على الهدى...»

5- Disbelief is blindness of the heart. «العمى»

6- Swift disbelief brings swift wrath. «فاستحبّوا... فاخذتهم»

7- Allah establishes the proof (completes the argument) before inflicting punishment. «فهدیناهم... فاخذتهم»

8- Humiliating the Prophets leads to humiliation on the Day of Judgment. «عذاب الهون»

9- Worse than heedlessness and disbelief is their persistence. «كانوا...»

10- The wrath or grace of Allah is the result of man's own actions. «یكسبون»

11- The key to salvation is faith (Iman) and piety (Taqwa). «و نجّینا الّذین آمنوا و كانوا یتّقون»

12- Faith (Iman) does not require renewal, but piety (Taqwa) is relevant at every moment and concerning every thought, action, word, and decision. («آمنوا» [they believed] is in the past tense, but «یتّقون» [they used to fear/be pious] is in the imperfect/present tense.)

Ayahs

وَيَوْمَ يُحْشَرُ أَعْدَآءُ ٱللَّهِ إِلَى ٱلنَّارِ فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ (۱۹)
The day the enemies of Allah are marched out toward the Fire, while they are in check, (19)
حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا مَا جَآءُوهَا شَهِدَ عَلَيْهِمْ سَمْعُهُمْ وَأَبْصَـٰرُهُمْ وَجُلُودُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۲٠)
when they come to it, their hearing, their eyes and their skins will bear witness against them concerning what they used to do. (20)

Key Points

«Yūza‘ūn» means to be held back (imprisoned). This means the first person is held and detained until the last person arrives, and then they are sent forth and divided towards the Hellfire.

Messages

1- Recounting the scenes of the Day of Judgment is a factor for warning (inzar) and education (tarbiyah). (The Quran commands the Prophet to remind people of such a day) «wa yawm...»

2- Those who are sent towards the Hellfire are those whose enmity towards Allah and the religion has been proven. «yuḥsharu a‘dā’u Allāhi ilā an-nār»

3- Man becomes an enemy of Allah through disbelief and obstinacy. «a‘dā’u Allāh»

4- In addition to tasting worldly punishment, the disbelievers will also be afflicted by the punishment of the Hellfire. «yuḥsharu... ilā an-nār»

5- The inhabitants of Hell are restrained from scattering and fleeing during their movement towards the Hellfire. «yūza‘ūn»

6- The body parts are aware of man's actions, because unless they are aware, they cannot bear witness on the Day of Judgment. «shahida ‘alayhim sam‘uhum...»

7- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical (bodily). «sam‘uhum wa abṣāruhum»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ لِجُلُودِهِمْ لِمَ شَهِدتُّمْ عَلَيْنَا ۖ قَالُوٓا۟ أَنطَقَنَا ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنطَقَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ وَهُوَ خَلَقَكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۲۱)
They will say to their skins, ‘Why did you bear witness against us?’ They will say, ‘We were given speech by Allah, who gave speech to all things. He created you the first time, and you are being brought back to Him. (21)

Key Points

On the Day of Judgment, there will be multiple witnesses, including: Allah, the Prophets, the earth, time, the angels, the body parts, and others... for each of whom there is an Ayah and a Hadith.

On the Day of Resurrection, every hidden thing shall become manifest And every criminal shall be disgraced by himself. The hand says, "I stole thus" The lip says, "I kissed thus." The eye says, "I cast a forbidden glance" The ear also says, "I heard evil speech."(25)

Existence possesses a type of speech. «انطق كل شى ء» (He made everything speak.) Allah says regarding Hell: «تقول هل من مزید»(26) Hell says: Do You have any more criminals? And regarding the earth and the sky, He says: «قالتا اتینا طائعین»(27) We come to You willingly.

The speech of water, the speech of dust, and the speech of clay Is perceptible to the senses of the people of the heart. All the particles of the earth and the sky Speak with you day and night. We are hearing, seeing, and conscious But with you, the strangers (uninitiated), we are silent.

(25) Mathnawi Ma'navi. (26) Qaf, 30. (27) Fussilat, 11.

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment is a scene of dispute and conflict between man and himself. «و قالوا لجلودهم»

2- Perhaps the disgrace of the sins committed by man using the skin of his body is greater. («و قالوا لجلودهم» and He did not say: «قالوا لابصارهم و...»)

3- The testimony of the body parts on the Day of Judgment is always against man and never in his favor. (Wherever the Quran speaks of the testimony of the body parts, it relates to sins). «لم شهدتم علینا»

4- The disbelievers accept the principle of sin but become upset by the testimony of their own body parts. «لم شهدتم علینا»

5- Testifying and speaking is a sign of the body parts' knowledge of man's actions. «انطقنا اللّه»

6- He who has the power to create also has the power to make things speak. «انطق كل شى ء و هو خلقكم»

7- The initial creation is the best proof of the possibility of the Resurrection. «خلقكم اول مرّة و الیه ترجعون»

Ayahs

وَمَا كُنتُمْ تَسْتَتِرُونَ أَن يَشْهَدَ عَلَيْكُمْ سَمْعُكُمْ وَلَآ أَبْصَـٰرُكُمْ وَلَا جُلُودُكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن ظَنَنتُمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَعْلَمُ كَثِيرًا مِّمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (۲۲)
You did not use to conceal yourselves [while perpetrating sinful acts] lest your hearing, your eyes, or your skins should bear witness against you, but you thought that Allah did not know most of what you did. (22)
وَذَٰلِكُمْ ظَنُّكُمُ ٱلَّذِى ظَنَنتُم بِرَبِّكُمْ أَرْدَىٰكُمْ فَأَصْبَحْتُم مِّنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (۲۳)
That misjudgement, which you entertained about your Lord ruined you. So you became losers.’ (23)

Messages

1- Sinners are unaware of the testimony of their body parts, and thus they dare to commit sin. «و ما كنتم تستترون...»

2- The witnessing of body parts in this world and their testimony in the Hereafter cannot be concealed or doubted, even though the sinners are unaware of this matter. «و ما كنتم تستترون...»

3- Belief that we are in the presence of Allah is the most important factor for human guidance and growth. «ظننتم ان اللّه لا یعلم كثیراً مما تعملون»

4- By reasoning and seeking help from Allah, we must strengthen and clarify our beliefs and avoid any kind of ill-opinion towards Him. «ظننتم بربّكم»

5- Having an ill-opinion towards the One under whose Lordship we are is extremely repugnant. «ظننتم بربّكم»

6- A wrong perception regarding Allah is the source of human loss and downfall. «ظننتم... أرداكم»

7- Conjecture and mere opinion have no value in matters of belief. «ظننتم بربّكم أرداكم»

Ayah

فَإِن يَصْبِرُوا۟ فَٱلنَّارُ مَثْوًى لَّهُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَسْتَعْتِبُوا۟ فَمَا هُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُعْتَبِينَ (۲٤)
Should they be patient, the Fire is their abode; and should they seek to propitiate [Allah], they will not be redeemed. (24)

Key Points

| «مثوى» (mathwā) means permanent abode, and «یستعتبوا» (yasta‘tibū) is derived from isti‘tāb, meaning the request for satisfaction and apology.

Messages

1- Patience or crying out has no effect for the disbelievers in Hell. «یصبروا - یستعتبوا»

2- Repentance and seeking pardon are effective only in this world. «فما هم من المعتبین»

3- On the Day of Judgment, some groups of people will be pardoned, and some groups will not. «فما هم من المعتبین»

Ayah

۞ وَقَيَّضْنَا لَهُمْ قُرَنَآءَ فَزَيَّنُوا۟ لَهُم مَّا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ وَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْقَوْلُ فِىٓ أُمَمٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ خَـٰسِرِينَ (۲٥)
We have assigned them companions who make their present [conduct] and future [state] seem decorous to them, and the word became due against them along with the nations of jinn and humans that passed away before them. They were indeed losers. (25)

Key Points

| «Qayyaḍnā» means encompassing and covering. The upper shell of an egg is called qayḍ. «Qurana'» is the plural of qarīn, meaning companion.

Messages

1- Corrupt friends cover a person's thoughts and personality. «Qayyaḍnā lahum quranā'»

2- A bad friend and companion is one of the divine punishments. «Qayyaḍnā lahum quranā'»

3- A bad friend, like Satan, makes a person's ugliness appear beautiful. «Zayyana lahumu ash-shayṭānu a‘mālahum»(28), «Fa-zayyanū lahum»

4- Deviants exploit the natural and instinctive desires of man. (Man instinctively loves beauty and goodness; deceivers exploit this fact and make bad deeds appear beautiful and good so that man easily accepts them.) «Zayyanū lahum mā bayna aydīhim wa mā khalfahum»

5- The result of seeing past sins as beautiful is that a person does not repent of his sins. «Mā khalfahum». And the result of seeing present deeds as good is that a person feels encouraged in his deviant behavior. «Fa-zayyanū lahum mā bayna aydīhim»

6- Man falls step by step: first, corrupt friends make ugliness appear beautiful to him, then he becomes deserving of divine wrath. «Ḥaqqa ‘alayhimu al-qawl»

7- Threat, punishment, and retribution are a permanent tradition (Sunnah) of Allah. «Ḥaqqa ‘alayhimu al-qawl fī umamin qad khalat min qablihim»

8- Jinn are accountable and choosing beings who, like humans, are subject to divine wrath, death, and mortality. «Khalat min qablihim mina al-jinni wa al-insi»

9- A bad friend is a source of loss for man. «Lahum quranā'... innahum kānū khāsirīn»(28) Al-Anfal, 48.

Ayahs

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَا تَسْمَعُوا۟ لِهَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ وَٱلْغَوْا۟ فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَغْلِبُونَ (۲٦)
The faithless say, ‘Do not listen to this Quran and hoot it down so that you may prevail [over the Apostle].’ (26)
فَلَنُذِيقَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّهُمْ أَسْوَأَ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۲۷)
We will surely make the faithless taste a severe punishment, and We will surely requite them by the worst of what they used to do. (27)
ذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ أَعْدَآءِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلنَّارُ ۖ لَهُمْ فِيهَا دَارُ ٱلْخُلْدِ ۖ جَزَآءًۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يَجْحَدُونَ (۲۸)
That is the requital of the enemies of Allah—the Fire! In it, they will have an everlasting abode, as a requital for their impugning Our signs. (28)

Key Points

The phrase "و الغوا فیه" (and make noise therein) means: divert the path of truth through every vain word or action, such as whistling, clapping, clamor, uproar, myth-making, creating doubt, and raising inappropriate questions.

Messages

1- Malicious propaganda against religion has always existed. "قال الّذین كفروا لا تسمعوا"

2- Those who lack logical arguments prevent people from listening to the logical arguments of others as well. "لا تسمعوا..."

3- Listening to the Quran possesses a special attraction and effectiveness that enemies fear. "لا تسمعوا لهذا القرآن"

4- The Prophet of Islam was constantly engaged in propagation (Dawah), and the disbelievers were distressed by these scenes. "لا تسمعوا"

5- The enemy both prevents people from listening to the Quran and strives to combat it through clamor and uproar. "لا تسمعوا... و الغوا"

6- The tool of the disbelievers is uttering nonsense (or vain talk). "و الغوا فیه"

7- Creating interference (or static/noise) in the word of truth is the work of the disbelievers. "و الغوا فیه"

8- The enemy takes action even with the mere possibility of victory. "لعلكم"

9- The enemy always promises victory to its followers. "تغلبون"

10- The punishment for those who spread malicious propaganda against the Quran, prevent people from listening to it, and command vain actions, is extremely severe. (The letter Lām at the beginning of the phrases "لنذیقنّ" and "لنجزینّهم", the letter Nūn at their end, and the word "شدید" are signs of the most intense type of torment).

11- The clamor and obstructionism of the disbelievers are worse than their disbelief and form the basis of their punishment. "لنجزینّهم اسوء الّذى..."

12- The enemy of the Quran is the enemy of Allah. "ذلك جزاء اعداء اللّه"

13- The punishment for conscious and continuous denial is eternal Hellfire. "لهم فیها دار الخلد جزاء بما كانوا بآیاتنا یجحدون"

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ أَرِنَا ٱلَّذَيْنِ أَضَلَّانَا مِنَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ نَجْعَلْهُمَا تَحْتَ أَقْدَامِنَا لِيَكُونَا مِنَ ٱلْأَسْفَلِينَ (۲۹)
The faithless will say, ‘Our Lord! Show us those who led us astray from among jinn and humans so that we may trample them under our feet, so that they may be among the lowermost!’ (29)

Messages

1- On the Day of Resurrection, the disbelievers will seek to identify the agents of their misguidance in order to take revenge on them. «ربّنا ارنا»

2- Disbelief is error and misguidance. «قال الّذین كفروا... اضلاّنا»

3- On the Day of Resurrection, the disbelievers will blame their crime on others. «اضلاّنا»

4- The Jinn and mankind play a role in the deviation of humanity. «اضلاّنا من الجنّ و الانس»

5- The misguiding leaders who were highly respected by others in this world, their followers will wish on the Day of Resurrection to trample them underfoot. «نجعلهما تحت اقدامنا»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوا۟ رَبُّنَا ٱللَّهُ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَقَـٰمُوا۟ تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ أَلَّا تَخَافُوا۟ وَلَا تَحْزَنُوا۟ وَأَبْشِرُوا۟ بِٱلْجَنَّةِ ٱلَّتِى كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ (۳٠)
Indeed those who say, ‘Our Lord is Allah!’ and then remain steadfast, the angels descend upon them, [saying,] ‘Do not fear, nor be grieved! Receive the good news of the paradise, which you have been promised. (30)

Key Points

We read a clear example of the descent of angels upon the believers in Surah Aal-i-Imran, Ayah 125, where He says: «بلى ان تصبروا و تتّقوا و یأتوكم من فورهم هذا یمددكم ربّكم بخمسة آلاف من الملائكة مسوّمین» Yes, if you, the fighters of the Battle of Badr, show steadfastness and piety, and the enemy comes to you immediately, Allah will aid you with five thousand marked angels. We also read a similar Ayah in Surah Al-Ahqaf, Ayahs 13 and 14: «ان الّذین قالوا ربّنا اللّه ثمّ استقاموا فلا خوف علیهم ولا هم یحزنون اولئك اصحاب الجنة خالدین فیها جزاء بما كانوا یعملون» Those who said: "Our Lord is 'Allah'," and then remained steadfast, there shall be no fear upon them, nor shall they grieve. They are the inhabitants of Paradise, abiding therein forever as a reward for what they used to do.(29)

29) As I write the commentary for this Ayah, it is the night of the seventh of Tir, 1379 (Solar Hijri calendar), and the nineteenth anniversary of the seventy-two martyrs of the Islamic Republic who were martyred simultaneously in the Munafeqin (Hypocrites) bombing incident in Tehran. They were a group of scholars, Islamic experts, Mujtahids (jurists), judges, representatives of the Islamic Consultative Assembly (Majlis), and among the best revolutionary and sincere figures who remained steadfast in the way of Allah and gave their lives in this path. May the peace of Allah be upon them and all the martyrs.

Messages

1- What makes faith fruitful and brings it to fruition is resistance; otherwise, many believers have met a bad end. «قالوا ربّنا اللّه ثمّ استقاموا»

2- Steadfastness is valuable when coupled with faith; otherwise, the disbelievers (Kuffar) also show steadfastness in their falsehood. «قالوا ربّنا اللّه ثمّ استقاموا»

3- Many accept Allah's role as Creator, but they involve others in the management of affairs (Tadbir); the important thing is for a person to remain steadfast in the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah, meaning they accept no law or program from anyone other than Him. «ربّنا اللّه ثمّ استقاموا»

4- Steadfastness is valuable when it is prolonged. «ثمّ استقاموا» (The word «ثمّ» (thumma) denotes time and long intervals.)

5- Through faith and resistance, man attracts the angels to himself. «تتنزّل علیهم الملائكة»

6- Angels descend upon non-Prophets as well. «تتنزّل علیهم الملائكة»

7- Angels descend upon steadfast believers. «تتنزّل علیهم الملائكة» And upon sinners, the devils descend. «هل انبّئكم على من تنزل الشیاطین تنزّل على كل افّاك اثیم»(30)

8- Steadfastness in the path of truth removes both the fear of the future. «لا تخافوا» And the grief and sorrow concerning the past. «لا تحزنوا»

9- The highest heavenly gift that the angels bring for the steadfast believers is spiritual and psychological tranquility. «لا تخافوا و لا تحزنوا و ابشروا»30) Shu'ara, 221.

Ayahs

نَحْنُ أَوْلِيَآؤُكُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَفِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَا تَشْتَهِىٓ أَنفُسُكُمْ وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَا تَدَّعُونَ (۳۱)
We are your friends in the life of this world and in the Hereafter, and you will have in it whatever your souls desire, and you will have in it whatever you ask for, (31)
نُزُلًا مِّنْ غَفُورٍ رَّحِيمٍ (۳۲)
as a hospitality from One all-forgiving, all-merciful.’ (32)

Key Points

Nuzul (نُزل) refers to something that is prepared for the reception of a guest upon their arrival and descent (nuzul).

Messages

  1. The steadfast believer has heavenly friends. «نحن اولیاءكم فى الحیاة الدنیا»

  2. Attention to the support and backing of the angels removes the anxiety of the believers. «لاتخافوا و لاتحزنوا نحن اولیاءكم»

  3. All human desires in Paradise are accessible and provided for. «و لكم فیها ما تشتهى...»

  4. In Paradise, both material pleasures are provided «تشتهى انفسكم» (what your souls desire) and spiritual requests «ما تدّعون» (what you ask for). In another place, He also says: «دعواهم فیها سبحانك اللّهم... و اخر دعواهم ان الحمدللّه» (31)

  5. All the pleasures of Paradise are a portion of Divine grace. «نزلا»

  6. Divine favors stem from His forgiveness and grace (not from human demand). «نُزلاً من غفور رحیم» (31) Yunus, 10.

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ قَوْلًا مِّمَّن دَعَآ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا وَقَالَ إِنَّنِى مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ (۳۳)
Who has a better call than him who summons to Allah and acts righteously and says, ‘Indeed I am one of the Muslims’? (33)

Key Points

In Ayah 18 of Surah Az-Zumar, Allah commanded the people to listen to the words and follow the best of them. This Ayah considers the most excellent word (sukhun ahsan) to be the call to Allah (da'wah ila Allah). Therefore, the duty of the people is to seek out, among the speakers, someone whose spirit is submitted to Allah, whose deed is righteous, and whose call is to the way of Allah.

Messages

  1. Propagating the religion is the best speech, and the best speakers in existence are the Prophets. «و من احسن قولاً...»

  2. The best speech is not that which is more scientific or more melodious, but rather that which invites people to Allah and is purposeful. «دعا الى اللّه»

  3. Speech is valuable only if its speaker is a person of action. «دعا الى اللّه و عمل صالحا» (Speakers without action are severely reprimanded. «لم تقولون مالا تفعلون»(32), «اتأمرون الناس بالبر و تنسون انفسكم»(33))

  4. A call is valuable if, in addition to action, it is accompanied by the vigor of submission and contentment. «انّنى من المسلمین» (32) Saff, 2. (33) Baqarah, 44.

Ayah

وَلَا تَسْتَوِى ٱلْحَسَنَةُ وَلَا ٱلسَّيِّئَةُ ۚ ٱدْفَعْ بِٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ فَإِذَا ٱلَّذِى بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُۥ عَدَٰوَةٌ كَأَنَّهُۥ وَلِىٌّ حَمِيمٌ (۳٤)
Good and evil [conduct] are not equal. Repel [evil] with what is best. [If you do so,] he between whom and you was enmity, will then be as though he were a sympathetic friend. (34)

Key Points

In the face of those who invite people to God, indifferent, unworthy, and ill-mannered individuals appear. If the preacher of religion does not deal with them with good manners and broad-mindedness, success will not be achieved. This Ayah recommends responding to evil deeds with good deeds and not being vengeful; just as in the supplication Makārim al-Akhlāq, Imam Zayn al-'Abidin عليه السلام asks Allah to grant him success so that in response to people backbiting him, he speaks well of them, overlooks their evil deeds, and associates with anyone who cuts ties with him. We frequently read examples of such conduct in the biography (Seerah) of the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and his infallible Household, which transformed the staunchest opponents into their supporters.

People fall into several categories: one who preserves his friend; one who makes his friend indifferent towards him; one who turns his friend into an enemy; one who turns an enemy into a friend.

Method of Dealing with Opponents

Opponents are divided into several groups, and each must be dealt with differently:

  • Sometimes individuals oppose the truth and show enmity due to ignorance and foolishness; this Ayah commands kindness and the best conduct (Ahsan). Other verses also address this:

    1. And when the ignorant address them (with improper speech), they reply with peaceful speech or 'Salām.' «و اذا خاطبهم الجاهلون قالوا سلاما»(34)
    2. And when they pass by vain and unpleasant talk, they pass by with dignity. «و اذا مرّوا باللغو مرّوا كراما»(35)

  • Sometimes the opposition of individuals is based on doubt and skepticism, and they must be dealt with through argumentation and the removal of ambiguities.

    • Doubt concerning Allah. «أفى اللّه شك...»(36)
    • Doubt and skepticism concerning the Day of Judgment and Resurrection. «ان كنتم فى ریب من البعث»(37)
    • Doubt concerning the descent of revelation and heavenly books upon the Prophet. «ان كنتم فى ریب مما نزلنا على عبدنا»(38)

  • Sometimes the opposition is based on envy, which must be dealt with through forbearance.

    • The brothers of Yusuf confessed their envy and mistake, and they were met with the promise of forgiveness from Prophet Yusuf. «انّا كنّا لخاطئین... لا تثریب علیكم الیوم»(39)
    • Habil, in response to the envy of his brother Qabil, said: If you stretch out your hand to kill me, I will (never) stretch out my hand to kill you. «لئن بسطت الىّ یدك لتقتلنى ما انا بباسط یدى الیك لاقتلك»(40)

  • Sometimes the opposition is due to maintaining position, comfort, and material enjoyments. In these cases, completing the argument (Itmām al-Hujjah) is sufficient, and the verses «ذرهم»(41) (Leave them to their own state) and «اعرض عنهم»(42) (Turn away from them and avoid them) are relevant.

  • Sometimes the opposition takes the form of sabotage, satire, and weakening people's faith. The following verses specify dealing with this group through severe confrontation, cutting ties, arrest, and sometimes execution:

    • «واغلظ علیهم»(43) Deal harshly and severely with the disbelievers.
    • «اذا سمعتم آیات اللّه یكفر بها و یستهزء بها فلاتقعدوا معهم حتّى یخوضوا فى حدیث غیره»(44) And when you hear the Verses of Allah being denied and ridiculed, you must not sit with them until they engage in other conversation.
    • «لا تتخدوا الیهود و النصارى اولیاء»(45) Do not take the Jews and the Christians as allies and guardians.
    • «اخذوا و قتّلوا تقتیلا»(46) The disbelievers should be seized and severely killed.

  • Sometimes their enmity involves taking up arms and armed struggle, regarding which the Quran states: «فاعتدوا علیه بمثل ما اعتدى علیكم...»(47) (Then commit aggression against him in the same way he committed aggression against you...).

Some have said in the interpretation of Ayah 40 of Surah Ash-Shura, «و جزاء سیّئة سیئة مثلها» (And the recompense of an evil deed is an evil deed like it), that the meaning is that if one responds to someone's evil deed with an evil deed, he is like him. Just as Ayah 43 of the same Surah states: «و لمن صبر و غفر انّ ذلك لمن عزم الامور» (But whoever is patient and forgives—indeed, that is among the matters requiring determination).

34) Al-Furqan, 63. 35) Al-Furqan, 72. 36) Ibrahim, 10. 37) Al-Hajj, 5. 38) Al-Baqarah, 23. 39) Yusuf, 91 - 92. 40) Al-Ma'idah, 28. 41) Al-An'am, 91 and Al-Hijr, 3. 42) Al-Ma'idah, 42, An-Nisa, 63 and 81. 43) At-Tawbah, 73. 44) An-Nisa, 140. 45) Al-Ma'idah, 51. 46) Al-Ahzab, 61. 47) Al-Baqarah, 194.

Messages

1- One of the ways of inviting to Allah is to de-escalate tension and repel the evil of others with goodness. «و من احسن قولاً ممّن دعا الى اللّه... ادفع بالتى هى احسن»

2- Do not think that the passage of time makes issues obsolete and everything is forgotten. (Positive and negative interactions are never equal, and their effects remain deep within the souls of individuals.) «لا تستوى الحسنة و لا السیّئة»

3- One practical example of inviting towards God (which was mentioned in the previous Ayah) is repelling people's evil deeds with good conduct. «ادفع بالتى هى احسن»

4- In dealing with opponents, good manners are not enough; moral offense (or proactive moral action) is necessary. «ادفع بالتى هى احسن» (Repelling the enemy's evil with a good method is not enough; rather, the best methods and the highest moral behaviors are required.)

Through love, thorns become roses. Through love, vinegar becomes fine wine.

5- True alchemy is not turning copper into gold, but rather conduct that transforms enmity into affection. «ادفع بالتى هى احسن» (On the Day of the Conquest of Mecca, the slogan of some Muslims was "Revenge, revenge," but the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Today is not the day of revenge, but the day of mercy and compassion.)

6- Broad-mindedness (patience/tolerance) is a condition for success in propagation (Dawah). «ادفع بالتى هى احسن -... كانّه ولىّ حمیم»

7- Attention to the result of the action raises human motivation. (If we know that the result of good conduct is the transformation of an enemy into a friend, our motivation for this action increases.) «ادفع بالتى هى احسن -... كانّه ولىّ حمیم»

8- In worthy conduct, even if the enemy does not become a friend, at least shame and embarrassment arise in him, compelling him toward friendly behavior. «كانّه ولىّ حمیم»

9- The value of a friend lies in his warmth (sincerity/closeness). «ولىّ حمیم»

Ayah

وَمَا يُلَقَّىٰهَآ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا۟ وَمَا يُلَقَّىٰهَآ إِلَّا ذُو حَظٍّ عَظِيمٍ (۳٥)
But none is granted it except those who are patient, and none is granted it except the greatly endowed. (35)

Key Points

| The great share and fortune, from the viewpoint of the worldly seekers, belongs to Qarun. «انه لذو حظّ عظیم»(48) But from the viewpoint of the Quran, the great share and fortune is for those who possess nobility of soul and breadth of heart. «و ما یلقّاها الاّ ذو حظّ عظیم»

48) Al-Qasas, 79.

Messages

1- Good conduct towards the unworthy and tolerance of opponents are specific to those who have previously engaged in self-building and practice. «و ما یلقّاها الا الّذین صبروا...» (The past tense of sabarū [صبروا] indicates that these individuals have engaged in self-building beforehand).

2- Reaching the peak of patience is not an easy task. (The word yulaqqāhā [یلقّاها] is repeated).

3- Vengeance and retaliation in ethical interactions are signs of lack of patience. «ادفع بالتى هى احسن... و ما یلقّاها الا الّذین صبروا»

4- Only the patient ones (the sābirūn) possess the great fortune. «الا الّذین صبروا... الاّ ذو حظّ عظیم»

Ayah

وَإِمَّا يَنزَغَنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ نَزْغٌ فَٱسْتَعِذْ بِٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۳٦)
Should an incitement from Satan prompt you [to ill feeling], seek the protection of Allah. Indeed, He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (36)

Key Points

The word nazgh (نزغ) means entering into an action with the intention of corruption, and it is also applied to the whispering (waswasah) and incitement of Satan. In this Ayah and the three preceding Ayahs, four points have been presented for those who call people to the path of Allah:(49)

In Ayah 32, He says: The best speech is calling towards Allah, provided that the caller himself is a person of action and submission to Allah's command.

In the next Ayah, He says: If you encounter opponents on the path of calling (to Allah), deal with them in the best manner so that you may change them, or act as if that opponent is your intimate friend and do not show any reaction.

In the Ayah after that, He said: This overlooking of hostilities and enmities requires patience and is specific to those who have received a great share of Allah's grace and favor.

In this Ayah, He says: If Satan whispers to you to deal harshly with the enemy, seek refuge in Allah.

Satan's whispering (nazgh) has various ways, means, and types:

Sometimes it is through frightening. «الشیطان یعدكم الفقر»(50) Sometimes it is through making things appear beautiful. «زیّن لهم الشیطان اعمالهم»(51) Sometimes it is through empty promises. «یعدهم و یمنیهم»(52) Sometimes it is through enmity and hatred. «یوقع بینكم العداوة و البغضاء»(53)

49) Tafsir-e Nemooneh. 50) Al-Baqarah, 268. 51) An-Naml, 24. 52) An-Nisa, 120. 53) Al-Ma'idah, 91.

Messages

1- Arousing the sense of vengeance in man is one of Satanic tricks, but responding to evil with good is among the Divine recommendations. «ادفع بالتى هى احسن... امّا ینزغنّك»

2- No one, not even the Prophet, is safe from the whispering of Satan. «و امّا ینزغنّك»

3- Satan's whispering is continuous. (Yanzaghannaka (ینزغنّك) is a present tense verb and signifies continuity).

4- Every element that causes whispering is Satanic. «من الشیطان نزغ»

5- Do not wait for Satan to dominate; seek refuge in Allah upon the appearance of the slightest whisper. «من الشیطان نزغ»

6- The cure for Satan's whispering is seeking refuge in Allah and repentance. «و امّا ینزغنّك فاستعذ باللّه»

7- Seeking refuge (isti'adhah) is one of the tools for the infallibility (ismah) of the prophets. «فاستعذ...»

8- One must seek refuge in Him Who encompasses all perfections, and is All-Hearing and All-Knowing. «فاستعذ باللّه» (The word Allah (اللّه) means the Sacred Essence Who possesses all perfections.)

9- Although Allah knows, we must express our weakness and dependence by tongue and heart. «فاستعذ... السمیع العلیم»

10- Seeking refuge in Allah does not go unanswered. «السمیع العلیم»

11- Only Allah is the Absolute All-Hearing and All-Knowing. «انّه هو السمیع العلیم»

Ayah

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِ ٱلَّيْلُ وَٱلنَّهَارُ وَٱلشَّمْسُ وَٱلْقَمَرُ ۚ لَا تَسْجُدُوا۟ لِلشَّمْسِ وَلَا لِلْقَمَرِ وَٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَهُنَّ إِن كُنتُمْ إِيَّاهُ تَعْبُدُونَ (۳۷)
Among His signs are night and day, and the sun and the moon. Do not prostrate to the sun, nor to the moon, but prostrate to Allah who created them, if it is Him that you worship. (37)

Key Points

Allah created existence, «خالق كل شى ء» (54), and has complete sovereignty over it, «للّه ملك السموات» (55), and all existence is His army. «للّه جنود السموات و الارض» (56) All are obedient to Him, «كلٌّ له قانتون» (57), and all are signs of His power, wisdom, and mercy. «و من آیاته...» And the return of all is towards Him. «الیه المنتهى» (58) In Ayah 32, the best speech was introduced as calling towards Allah, and this Ayah explains the path of calling towards Allah. Furthermore, this Ayah contains an obligatory prostration (Sajdah Wajib). Night and day are among the Divine signs and indicators of Allah's power: the tranquility of the night and its succession by the day, the timing and variation (increase and decrease) of the two, and their suitability for human sleep and rest. The blessings of the day include: the growth of plants and animals, the evaporation of water followed by the formation of clouds and rain, the bearing of fruit by trees, the storage of energy, the emergence of movement, travel and journey, the share the earth has in its own movement, the distance between the sun and the earth, and the space surrounding the earth which controls the sun's heat. Likewise, the existence of the moon, which is the lamp of the night and a natural and universal calendar for everyone, as well as the movements of the moon in different phases (manazil) and its role in the ebb and flow (tides) of the seas—all of these are signs of the Lord and indicators of His power.

54) Zumar, 62. 55) Aal-i Imran, 189. 56) Al-Fath, 4. 57) Al-Baqarah, 116. 58) An-Najm, 42.

Messages

1- Night and day are among the signs of Divine power, wisdom, and mercy. «و من آیاته الیل...»

2- Nature is the best classroom for knowing Allah (Theology). «و من آیاته الّیل و النّهار و...»

3- Even the most fruitful beings in existence are not worthy of worship. «لا تسجدوا للشمس»

4- In educating individuals, while preventing them from wrong paths, also explain the correct paths with reasoning. «لا تسجدوا للشمس... و اسجدوا للّه...»

5- If the sun, despite its grandeur, is not worthy of worship, then the status of these small and ineffective idols is clear. «لا تسجدوا للشمس»

6- The Creator of existence is worthy of worship. The created cannot be the object of worship (Ma'bood). «خلقهنّ»

7- Monotheism in Creation (Tawhid al-Khaliqiyyah) is the proof for Monotheism in Worship (Tawhid al-Ibādah). «خلقهنّ... ایّاه تعبدون»

Ayah

فَإِنِ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فَٱلَّذِينَ عِندَ رَبِّكَ يُسَبِّحُونَ لَهُۥ بِٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ وَهُمْ لَا يَسْـَٔمُونَ ۩ (۳۸)
But if they disdain [the worship of Allah], those who are near your Lord glorify Him night and day, and they are not wearied. (38)

Messages

1- Refusal to prostrate (sajdah) and worship is a clear example of arrogance (istikbar). «و اسجدوا للّه... فان استكبروا»

2- Arrogance (takabbur) is an obstacle to servitude (worship). «و اسجدوا... فان استكبروا...»

3- Break the pride of the arrogant by presenting superior role models. If arrogant humans do not prostrate, it is not important, because the angels prostrate without weariness. «فالّذین عند ربّك یسبّحون»

4- Allah consoles His Prophet against the arrogant ones. «فالّذین عند ربّك»

5- Even if all humans abandon divine worship, the angels and the universe remain submissive to Allah. «فان استكبروا فالّذین عند ربّك یسبحون»

How can the sea become impure by a dog's muzzle? How can the sun be extinguished by a puff of breath? If all beings in existence turn disbelievers, No dust shall settle upon the hem of His Majesty.

6- Glorification (tasbih) is the work of the angels, and those on earth who glorify are similar to the angels. «یسبّحون له»

7- Glorification (tasbih) is the peak of worship. (In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about worship; in this Ayah, it is about glorification.) «ایاه تعبدون... یسبّحون له»

8- Night and day are means for knowing Allah and worshipping Him, not that they themselves should be worshipped. «یسبّحون له بالّیل...»

9- Worship during the night is emphasized more strongly. (The name of the night precedes the day.) «بالّیل و النّهار»

10- The angels glorify (Allah) day and night. (The word «یسبّحون» indicates continuity, and furthermore, the words night and day also indicate permanence.)

11- The angels possess sincerity (ikhlas) in glorifying Allah, «یسبّحون له...» and He did not say: «یسبّحونه». Therefore, we too should possess sincerity.

Ayah

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ أَنَّكَ تَرَى ٱلْأَرْضَ خَـٰشِعَةً فَإِذَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهَا ٱلْمَآءَ ٱهْتَزَّتْ وَرَبَتْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحْيَاهَا لَمُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۳۹)
Among His signs, is that you see the earth desolate; but when We send down water upon it, it stirs and swells. Indeed, He who revives it will also revive the dead. Indeed, He has power over all things. (39)

Key Points

In Surah Al-Hajj, the earth is mentioned using the term hāmidah (هامدة), meaning dry: «ترى الارض هامدة فاذا انزلنا علیها الماء اهتّزت و ربت و انبتت... و انّه یحیى الموتى و انه على كل شى ء قدیر»(59). But here (in this Surah), He says khāshi‘ah (خاشعة - submissive). Perhaps the word khushū‘ (humility/submissiveness) in this Ayah is a kind of taunt directed at the arrogant ones who were mentioned in the previous Ayah.

59) Al-Hajj, 5.

Messages

1- The soft earth grows with the descent of rain, but hard hearts do not change with the descent of the verses (Ayat). «ترى الارض خاشعة» And this is a warning to the arrogant and the atheists that they are lower than the earth.

2- Allah carries out His work through natural factors. «انزلنا...»

3- "A handful is a sample of the heap" (A small example proves the whole). He who revives the dead earth can revive the dead on the Day of Resurrection. «لمحیى الموتى»

4- The reason for denying the Resurrection is heedlessness regarding the Divine power. «انه على كل شى ء قدیر»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُلْحِدُونَ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِنَا لَا يَخْفَوْنَ عَلَيْنَآ ۗ أَفَمَن يُلْقَىٰ فِى ٱلنَّارِ خَيْرٌ أَم مَّن يَأْتِىٓ ءَامِنًا يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ مَا شِئْتُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (٤٠)
Those who abuse Our signs are not hidden from Us. Is someone who is cast in the Fire better off, or someone who arrives safely on the Day of Resurrection? Act as you wish; indeed He watches what you do. (40)

Key Points

The word ilhad (إلحاد) comes from lahd (لحد), meaning a cavity that deviates to one side. Mulhid (ملحد) means one who deviates from the truth.

Messages

1- It is Allah's tradition to grant respite. Although the actions of the deviants are not hidden from Allah, He leaves them to their own devices so they may continue their deviation. «یلحدون... لا یخفون علینا»

2- The deviants will be cast into the Fire (Hell). «یلقى فى النار»

3- The people of Paradise approach Paradise in peace and glory, but the people of the Fire are forcibly cast into Hell. «یلقى فى النار - یأتى آمناً»

4- The most important Divine gift to the believers is security (safety). «یأتى آمنا»

5- Letting go and granting freedom is not always beneficial for man. «اعملوا ما شئتم»

6- Allah's supervision is the greatest threat to the deviants and a source of comfort for the people of faith. In this Ayah, Allah's supervision is mentioned twice. «لا یخفون علینا - انّه بما تعملون بصیر»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِٱلذِّكْرِ لَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ ۖ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَكِتَـٰبٌ عَزِيزٌ (٤۱)
Those who reject the Reminder when it comes to them... Indeed, it is an august Book: (41)
لَّا يَأْتِيهِ ٱلْبَـٰطِلُ مِنۢ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِۦ ۖ تَنزِيلٌ مِّنْ حَكِيمٍ حَمِيدٍ (٤۲)
falsehood cannot approach it, at present or in future, a [revelation gradually] sent down from One all-wise, all-laudable. (42)
مَّا يُقَالُ لَكَ إِلَّا مَا قَدْ قِيلَ لِلرُّسُلِ مِن قَبْلِكَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو مَغْفِرَةٍ وَذُو عِقَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (٤۳)
Nothing is said to you except what has already been said to the apostles before you. Indeed your Lord is One who forgives and also metes out a painful retribution. (43)

Key Points

Ayah 43 can be interpreted in two ways: First, that every matter revealed to you through revelation is the same matter that was revealed to previous prophets. Second, that whatever inappropriate speech the opponents direct toward you is not a new thing, but rather the same words they audaciously spoke to previous prophets.

In the noble commentary Majma‘ al-Bayan, the following meanings are given regarding the Qur’an being ‘Aziz (Mighty/Inviolable): A) No one can produce anything like it. B) Allah has honored it by protecting it from distortion (tahrif). C) It contains the most solid expressions. D) It is worthy for people to hold it dear.

The title ‘Aziz (Mighty) among the heavenly books is exclusive to the Qur’an. «و انه لكتاب عزیز»

Imam Sadiq and Imam Baqir علیهما السلام said: There is no falsehood in the reports the Qur’an narrates about the past or in the prophecies it makes about the future.(60) In another tradition from Imam Baqir علیه السلام, we read that there is nothing in the previous heavenly books, such as the Torah, the Gospel, and the Psalms, that invalidates the Qur’an, nor will any book be written in the future that defeats the Qur’an.(61) «من بین یدیه و لا من خلفه»

60) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 61) Tafsirs Nur al-Thaqalayn and Qummi.

Messages

1- The Qur’an is a source of Dhikr (remembrance and admonition). «كفروا بالذكر» (It reminds man of what he has become heedless of and forgotten.)

2- The disbelief of a group of people does not cause the heavenly book to fail. «لكتاب عزیز»

3- The Qur’an informs about its own future. The inviolability (‘Izzat) of the Qur’an and its protection from distortion throughout history, despite so many enemies, is a piece of unseen news (a prophecy). «عزیز - لا یأتیه الباطل»

4- There is no falsehood whatsoever in the Qur’an. (Neither lies, nor contradictions, nor distortion, nor...). « لا یأتیه الباطل»

5- All past and future conspiracies aimed at fighting the Qur’an are ineffective. «عزیز... لا یأتیه...»

6- False interpretations are the understanding of some people regarding the Qur’an, but the Qur’an itself remains firm. «تنزیل من حكیم»

7- The Qur’an, which is from the Wise God, is wise and firm. (Its content, wording, method of revelation, and its effect are all firm and solid). «تنزیل من حكیم»

8- Allah is Wise. «تنزیل من حكیم...» The Qur’an is also wise. «یس و القرآن الحكیم»(62) And the Prophet is the teacher of wisdom. «یعلّمهم الكتاب و الحكمة»(63)

9- The Qur’an is a manifestation of Divine Wisdom and proof of Allah’s worthiness of praise. «حكیم حمید»

10- Even prophets need consolation, and this Ayah is a form of consolation for the Prophet. «ما یقال لك»

11- The reaction of opponents toward the prophets is uniform. «ما یقال لك الاّ...»

12- Religious leaders must endure the insults of opponents. «ما یقال لك الاّ قد قیل...»

13- The path of repentance is always open. «لذو مغفرة»

14- Attention to Allah’s Mercy and punishment prepares the ground for human repentance. «لذو مغفرة و ذو عقاب»

15- Warning and hope must be presented side by side. «ذو مغفرة... ذو عقاب»

16- Allah’s Mercy precedes His Wrath. (Forgiveness is mentioned first, then punishment).

17- Forgiveness and punishment are among the attributes of Lordship (Rububiyyah). «ان ربّك لذو مغفرة و ذو عقاب الیم»

62) Ya Sin, 1-2. 63) Al ‘Imran, 164.

Ayah

وَلَوْ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ قُرْءَانًا أَعْجَمِيًّا لَّقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَا فُصِّلَتْ ءَايَـٰتُهُۥٓ ۖ ءَا۬عْجَمِىٌّ وَعَرَبِىٌّ ۗ قُلْ هُوَ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ هُدًى وَشِفَآءٌ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ فِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِمْ وَقْرٌ وَهُوَ عَلَيْهِمْ عَمًى ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يُنَادَوْنَ مِن مَّكَانٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (٤٤)
Had We made it a non-Arabic Quran, they would have said, ‘Why have not its signs been articulated?’ ‘What! A non-Arabian [scripture] and an Arabian [prophet]!?’ Say, ‘It is guidance and healing for those who have faith. As for those who are faithless, there is a deafness in their ears and it is lost to their sight: [to them it is as if] they were called from a distant place.’ (44)

Key Points

The word 'A'jami' (أعجمى) comes from 'ujmah' (عُجمه), meaning unclear or incomprehensible. Since non-Arabic languages were incomprehensible to the Arabs, they referred to non-Arabic languages as 'A'jamiyy.

Messages

1- The enemy never ceases making excuses; if the Qur'an is Arabic, they say: Our ears are heavy «فى آذانهم وقر», and if it were non-Arabic ('A'jami'), they would say: Why are its verses not detailed/clear? «لولا فصّلت ایاته»

2- The condition for benefiting from the guidance and healing of the Qur'an is faith. «للّذین آمنوا هدىً و شفاء»

3- The Qur'an is the cure for moral, familial, social, and other illnesses. «شفاء»

4- Whoever closes his eyes before the sun of the Qur'an is as if he is blind. «و هو علیهم عمىً»

5- Readiness is necessary for being influenced. A person whose spiritual vessel is open and facing upward absorbs even a single drop of rain from the sky, but a person whose spiritual vessel is closed and facing downward absorbs nothing, even if he plunges into the sea of knowledge. Allah says regarding those who are beside the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله but lack the readiness to accept: It is as if they are being addressed from a distant place. «ینادون من مكان بعید»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ فَٱخْتُلِفَ فِيهِ ۗ وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَفِى شَكٍّ مِّنْهُ مُرِيبٍ (٤٥)
Certainly We gave Moses the Book, but differences arose about it; and were it not for a prior decree of your Lord, judgement would have been made between them, for they are in grave doubt concerning it. (45)

Messages

1- Familiarity with the history of the prophets is a source of comfort for the Prophet and the Muslims. «و لقد آتینا...»

2- The Torah has been subjected to dispute and distortion. «و لقد آتینا موسى الكتاب فاختلف فیه»

3- Granting respite to sinners is among the attributes of Lordship, so that people might repent and achieve growth. «سبقت من ربّك»

4- The tradition of Allah is to grant respite to sinners; otherwise, the life record of every person would be closed with the first deviation. «لو لا كلمة... لقضى بینهم»

5- Doubt must be a prelude to investigation, questioning, and reaching certainty, not a means for finding excuses and harboring suspicion. «لفى شك منه مریب»

Ayah

مَّنْ عَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَلِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَنْ أَسَآءَ فَعَلَيْهَا ۗ وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِظَلَّـٰمٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ (٤٦)
Whoever acts righteously, it is for [the benefit of] his own soul, and whoever does evil, it is to its detriment, and your Lord is not tyrannical to His servants. (46)

Key Points

Since even a small injustice, when attributed to the Great God, is considered equivalent to a great injustice, the word "ظلاّم" (Thallām) is used, which means one who commits excessive injustice.

Search for the root of bitter and sweet events in your own actions. «مَن عمل صالحا فلنفسه و من اساء فعلیها» And do not attribute them to others:

Do not attribute them to Allah. As the Quran says: When his Lord tests and honors man, he says: My Lord has honored me, and in other circumstances, he says: My Lord has humiliated me. «ربّ اكرمن... ربّ اهانن» But the Quran answers: It is not so; rather, your humiliation is because you did not care for the orphan and the needy. «كلا بل لا تكرمون الیتیم»(64)

Do not attribute them to people. As the oppressed (Mustad'afūn) will say to the arrogant (Mustakbirūn) on the Day of Judgment: «لولا انتم لكنا مؤمنین»(65) You are to blame; if it were not for you, we would have been believers.

Do not attribute them to ancestors. When the deviated are told to follow what Allah has revealed, they say: «نتّبع ما وجدنا علیه آباءنا»(66) We follow what we found our fathers upon.

Do not attribute them to the Prophets. As the disbelievers considered the Prophets to be the cause of misfortune and told them: «انّا تطیّرنا بكم»(67) We consider your presence ominous, and all our misfortunes come from you.

Do not attribute them to parents. Just as the Quran considers man to possess free will and sets limits for obedience to parents, saying: «وان جاهداك لتشرك بى ما لیس لك به علم فلاتطعهما»(68) If your parents strive to make you associate partners with Me concerning that of which you have no knowledge, never obey them.

Do not attribute them to deities. As the Quran says: «لا ینفعكم شیئاً و لا یضرّكم»(69) These idols bring you neither benefit nor harm.

Do not attribute them to gender, being male or female. Because the Quran says: «مَن عمل صالحاً»(70) meaning the principle is the action itself, regardless of who performs it.

Do not attribute them to Satan. As Satan says: Do not blame me or reproach me, but rather blame yourselves, «دعوتكم فاستجبتم»(71) It is you who accepted my invitation freely and knowingly.

Do not attribute them to a companion. As on the Day of Judgment, the deviated will turn to one another and say: It was you who came to us with oaths and power (claiming good and righteousness), but they reply: «بل لم تكونوا مؤمنین»(72) Rather, you yourselves were not believers.

Do not attribute them to the environment. Just as the wife of Pharaoh was in an environment of tyranny, fear, and wealth, yet she was not attracted by any of it. «امرئة فرعون»(73). Likewise, when the angels seize the souls of some who blame the environment, they say: «ألم تك ارض اللّه واسعة»(74) Was not the earth of Allah vast? Why did you not migrate and remain in a state of weakness (Istid'āf)?

64) Fajr, 15 - 20. 65) Saba', 31. 66) Luqmān, 21. 67) Yā Sīn, 18. 68) Luqmān, 15. 69) Al-Anbiyā', 66. 70) Fuṣṣilat, 46. 71) Ibrāhīm, 22. 72) Aṣ-Ṣāffāt, 28. 73) At-Taḥrīm, 11. 74) An-Nisā', 97.

Ayah

۞ إِلَيْهِ يُرَدُّ عِلْمُ ٱلسَّاعَةِ ۚ وَمَا تَخْرُجُ مِن ثَمَرَٰتٍ مِّنْ أَكْمَامِهَا وَمَا تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنثَىٰ وَلَا تَضَعُ إِلَّا بِعِلْمِهِۦ ۚ وَيَوْمَ يُنَادِيهِمْ أَيْنَ شُرَكَآءِى قَالُوٓا۟ ءَاذَنَّـٰكَ مَا مِنَّا مِن شَهِيدٍ (٤۷)
On Him devolves the knowledge of the Hour, and no fruit emerges from its covering and no female conceives or delivers except with His knowledge. On the day when He will call out to them, ‘Where are My ‘partners’?’ They will say, ‘We informed You that there is no witness amongst us.’ (47)

Key Points

«أكمام» is the plural of «كم», meaning something that covers the fruit. «كُم» (with a Dammah on the Kâf) refers to the sleeve which covers the hand, and «كُمة» refers to something that covers the head, like a skullcap. «آذنّاك» is derived from «ایذان», meaning announcement or declaration.

It is mentioned in the occasion of the revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of this Ayah that people asked the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: When is the Hour (Qiyamah)? This Ayah was revealed and answered: Knowledge of it belongs exclusively to Allah. Knowledge is of several types: some that all people can know, some that Allah teaches to His Prophets and Awliyâ (saints), and some that He has reserved exclusively for Himself, concerning which we recite in supplication: «و اسئلك بكل اسم اصطفیته من علمك لنفسك و استأثرت به فى علم الغیب عندك»(75) “O God! By the right of that knowledge which is exclusive to You,” and the knowledge of the time of Qiyamah is of the third type.

The Calls (Nidâ') of Qiyamah: The call of peace to the people of Paradise: «نادوا اصحاب الجنّة ان سلام علیكم»(76) The call of the people of Paradise to the people of the Fire: that we have found what our Lord promised us, what about you? «و نادى اصحاب الجنّة اصحاب النّار أن قد وجدنا ما وعدنا ربّنا»(77) The call of the people of the Fire for water and other provisions. «نادى اصحاب النّار اصحاب الجنّة أن أفیضوا علینا من الماء او مما رزقكم اللّه»(78) The call of Allah to the polytheists: Where are My partners? «ینادیهم فیقول این شركائى»(79) The call of the hypocrites to the believers: We were with you in the world, help us. «ینادونهم الم نكن معكم»(80)

75) Bihar, Vol. 88, p. 175. 76) Al-A'raf, 46. 77) Al-A'raf, 44. 78) Al-A'raf, 50. 79) Al-Qasas, 62. 80) Al-Hadid, 14.

Messages

1- In upbringing (Tarbiya), fear and hope are necessary, and this is achieved when man does not know the time of the occurrence of the Hour. «الیه یردّ علم السّاعة»

2- Allah is aware even of the minute details of existence. «اكمامها»

3- Allah's knowledge is not limited merely to the gender of the fetus, but encompasses all characteristics of the fetus. «و ما تحمل من أنثى و...»

4- We should turn to a deity concerning whom we can provide an answer (on the Day of Judgment). «این شركائى»

5- The polytheists will be questioned repeatedly and will declare their own helplessness and the helplessness of their deities. «آذنّاك»

6- One of the spiritual torments on the Day of Qiyamah is the shame felt in giving an answer. «آذنّاك ما منّا مِن شهید»

Ayahs

وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَدْعُونَ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَظَنُّوا۟ مَا لَهُم مِّن مَّحِيصٍ (٤۸)
What they used to invoke before has forsaken them, and they know there is no escape for them. (48)
لَّا يَسْـَٔمُ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ مِن دُعَآءِ ٱلْخَيْرِ وَإِن مَّسَّهُ ٱلشَّرُّ فَيَـُٔوسٌ قَنُوطٌ (٤۹)
Man is never wearied of supplicating for good, and should any ill befall him, he becomes hopeless and despondent. (49)

Key Points

«محیص» به معناى گریز یا محل گریز است. بعضى كلمه ى «یؤوس» و «قنوط» را مرادف هم گرفته اند، ولى فخر رازى مى گوید: یأس، نومیدى در درون است و قنوط، اظهار نومیدى به دیگران است. شاید مراد از خیر در آیه ثروت دنیا باشد چنان كه در جاى دیگر مى خوانیم: اگر كسى از دنیا رفت و خیرى بر جا گذاشت، چنین و چنان كنید. «ان ترك خیراً الوصیة»(81)

81) Al-Baqarah, 180.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the manifestation of the Truth is such that all false deities fade away and vanish, and their futility becomes clear even to the polytheists. «ضلّ عنهم ما كانوا یدعون»

2- On the Day of Judgment, the polytheist sees his past as void and his future as a dead end. «ضلّ... من قبل... من محیص»

3- Man does not become satiated or tired of seeking good (blessings); due to the greed, avarice, and desire for excess that are part of his nature, he desires all good things for himself. «لا یسأم الانسان من دعاء الخیر»

4- The misguided person lacks capacity (is impatient) and considers himself to be in a dead end upon the first encounter with hardship. «یؤوس قنوط»

Ayah
