وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْنَـٰهُ رَحْمَةً مِّنَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِ ضَرَّآءَ مَسَّتْهُ لَيَقُولَنَّ هَـٰذَا لِى وَمَآ أَظُنُّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ قَآئِمَةً وَلَئِن رُّجِعْتُ إِلَىٰ رَبِّىٓ إِنَّ لِى عِندَهُۥ لَلْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ فَلَنُنَبِّئَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِمَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ غَلِيظٍ (٥٠)
If, after distress has befallen him, We let him have a taste of Our mercy, he says, ‘This is my due! I do not think the Hour will ever come, and in case I am returned to my Lord, I will indeed have the best [reward] with Him.’ So We will surely inform the faithless about what they have done, and will surely make them taste a harsh punishment. (50)

Key Points

Worldliness is a cause of heedlessness and sometimes the denial of the Resurrection (Ma'ad). In Surah Al-Kahf, Ayah 35, we read that a person entered his garden and orchard, and when he saw its greenery, he said: This garden will never perish, «ما اظنّ ان تبید هذه ابدا» (I do not think that this will ever perish), and there is no Resurrection, «ما اظنّ الساعة قائمة» (I do not think the Hour will occur), and if I am returned in the Hereafter, I will certainly be given something better than this garden and orchard «لاجدنّ خیراً منها...» (I will surely find something better than it...). In this Ayah (the current one being discussed), we also read: Some individuals, as soon as they attain a mercy, deny the Resurrection and say: Assuming there is a Resurrection, the best things will belong to us.

Messages

1- The misguided person is narrow-minded (lacks capacity) and arrogant, and at the first stage of attaining a blessing, he behaves improperly (becomes intoxicated by it). «لئن اذقناه رحمة... لیقولنّ هذا لى» (If We let him taste mercy... he will surely say: This is mine.) (Just as upon the first encounter with evil, he becomes inwardly despairing «یؤوس قنوط» and cries out. «اذا مسّه الشر جزوعا»(82))

2- Man has no claim upon Allah, and whatever blessing he attains is due to His grace. «رحمةً منّا» (A mercy from Us.)

3- The source of mercy is from Him «رحمة منّا», but the source of hardships is the actions and characteristics of man himself. «ضراء مسّته» (Hardship touches him), and He did not say: «ضراءً منّا» (Hardship from Us).

4- The bitterness and sweetness of the world are slight; they are only to the extent of tasting and touching. «اذقنا - مسّته» (We let him taste - touches him.)

5- The disbelieving person is selfish and considers his successes to be his absolute right. «هذا لى» (This is mine.) (Just as Qarun used to say: My wealth is due to knowledge I possess. «على علم عندى»(83))

6- Prosperity and blessing are not signs of merit, entitlement, or a good ultimate outcome. (The Quran criticizes the one who considers himself worthy in this Ayah). «هذا لى»

7- The more a person focuses on himself, the more heedless he becomes of the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad). «هذا لى و ما اظنّ السّاعة قائمة» (This is mine, and I do not think the Hour will occur.)

8- The slightest blessing leads some people to falsehood, to the extent that they say: The blessing is my right. «هذا لى» There is no Resurrection. «و ما اظن الساعة قائمة» Assuming there is a Resurrection, the best things will be for me. «ان لى عنده للحسنى»

9- The disbelievers have no logic or reasoning for themselves, and their belief is based on conjecture. «و ما اظن الساعة» (And I do not think the Hour...)

10- Doubt and uncertainty regarding the Resurrection are signs of disbelief (Kufr). «و ما اظن الساعة قائمة... الّذین كفروا» (And I do not think the Hour will occur... those who disbelieved.)

11- On the Day of Resurrection, the reality and inner nature of deeds will be revealed to man. «فلننبّئن الّذین كفروا بما عملوا» (We will surely inform those who disbelieved of what they did.)

12- In the Hereafter, the crime of the transgressor will first be made known to him, and then he will be punished. «فلننبّئن... فلنذیقنّهم» (We will surely inform... then We will surely make them taste.)

13- He who states his false assumption firmly and decisively must receive a firm and decisive response. The response to «انّ لى عنده للحسنى» (Surely, I will have the best with Him) is the statement «فلننبّئن الّذین كفروا» (We will surely inform those who disbelieved) and «فلنذیقنّهم من عذاب غلیظ» (and We will surely make them taste a severe punishment.)

(82) Al-Ma'arij, 20. (83) Al-Qasas, 78.

Ayah

وَإِذَآ أَنْعَمْنَا عَلَى ٱلْإِنسَـٰنِ أَعْرَضَ وَنَـَٔا بِجَانِبِهِۦ وَإِذَا مَسَّهُ ٱلشَّرُّ فَذُو دُعَآءٍ عَرِيضٍ (٥۱)
When We bless man, he is disregardful and turns aside; but when an ill befalls him, he makes protracted supplications. (51)

Key Points

The word na'ā (نَأى) means to distance oneself; and whenever it is used with the word jānib (جانب), it is a metaphor for arrogance and pride. Na'ā bi-jānibihi (نأى بجانبه) means he became arrogant due to blessing and prosperity and haughtily withdrew himself. The word ‘arīḍ (عریض) means wide and is a metaphor for extensive supplication (du'ā). Question: In Ayah 49, we read: «اذا مسّه الشرّ كان یؤوسا» (When evil touches him, he is in despair). But in this Ayah, He says: «اذا مسّه الشرّ فذو دعاء عریض» (When evil touches him, he is one of extensive supplication). The question is, how does a despairing person supplicate? Answer: Commentators have given different answers to this question: In Tafsir Ruh al-Bayan, it is stated thus: Some people despair during affliction, while others supplicate. Some have said in response to this question: Man despairs of ordinary means but makes requests from Allah. However, the tone of the Ayah is one of reproach, and despairing of ordinary means while turning to Allah is a good deed and does not warrant reproach. In Tafsir Namūneh, it is stated that man initially supplicates but gradually despairs, and this supplication and despair occur in two stages. Perhaps it can be said that the intent of du'ā (supplication) is not seeking help from Allah, but rather the despair itself, which is expressed in the form of impatience, screaming, and lamentation. Both Ayahs address the same issue, but in one place, He says the person is despairing, and in another place, He says that due to despair, he screams and shouts, similar to the Ayah: «اذا مسّه الشر جزوعا»

Messages

1- Prosperity or hardship in the field of life is for the manifestation and emergence of human characteristics and his spiritual afflictions. «اذا انعمنا... و اذا مسّه الشّر»

2- Prosperity is a cause of heedlessness. «انعمنا... اعرض»

3- Blessings are from Allah «انعمنا», but bitter events are the result of man's own actions or bad characteristics. «مسّه الشّر» (Evil is not attributed to Allah).

4- An uncultivated person has little capacity (is impatient). «اذا مسّه الشرّ فذو دعاء عریض»

5- The problems and hardships of life are minor compared to the blessings bestowed upon him by Allah. (For blessing, the word «انعمنا» is used, but for evil, the word «مسّه» is used, which means a simple touch.)

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن كَانَ مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ كَفَرْتُم بِهِۦ مَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنْ هُوَ فِى شِقَاقٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (٥۲)
Say, ‘Tell me, if it is from Allah and you disbelieve in it, who will be more astray than those who are in extreme defiance.’ (52)

Messages

1- Allah teaches His Prophet the method of dialogue with obstinate individuals. «قل أرایتم ان...»

2- The rational person also avoids potential harms. (If the Qur’an is from Allah, what will those who disbelieved do?) «ان كان من عنداللّه... من اضل...»

3- The root of disbelief (Kufr) is hostility, obstinacy, and opposition to the truth. «كفرتم... شقاق بعید»

Ayahs

سَنُرِيهِمْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا فِى ٱلْـَٔافَاقِ وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۗ أَوَلَمْ يَكْفِ بِرَبِّكَ أَنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدٌ (٥۳)
Soon We shall show them Our signs in the horizons and in their own souls until it becomes clear to them that He is the Real. Is it not sufficient that your Lord is witness to all things? (53)
أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ فِى مِرْيَةٍ مِّن لِّقَآءِ رَبِّهِمْ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّهُۥ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ مُّحِيطٌۢ (٥٤)
Indeed, they are in doubt about the encounter with their Lord! Indeed, He embraces all things! (54)

Key Points

Knowledge of Allah (Gnosis) has stages: sometimes we deduce the Agent from the effects. («آیاتنا فى الآفاق و فى انفسهم» - Our signs in the horizons and within themselves.) And sometimes we find Him directly and set aside the intermediaries. («اولم یكف بربّك انّه على كلّ شى ء شهید» - Is it not sufficient that your Lord is a Witness over all things?) Just as He Himself is a Witness to His own Oneness. «شهداللّه انّه لا اله الاّ هو»(۸۴)

When have You departed from the heart that I should long for You? When have You been hidden that I should seek to find You? You have emerged with a hundred thousand manifestations, So that I may gaze upon You with a hundred thousand eyes.

Imam Husayn عليه السلام says in the Dua of Arafa: «متى غبت حتّى تحتاج الى دلیل یدلّ علیك... عمیت عین لا تراك» (When were You absent that You should need a proof to guide to You... Blind is the eye that does not see You.) Is there a manifestation for others that is not for You? When were You absent that You should need a proof? Blind be the eye that does not see You.

Why do we doubt the Resurrection (Ma'ad)? Does He not know what we have done so that He may give recompense and reward? «انه على كلّ شى ء شهید» Or can He not create again? «انّه على كلّ شى ء قدیر» Yes, the goal of these doubts is to open the way for transgression and immorality (fisq and fujur). «یرید الانسان لیفجر امامه یسئل ایّان یوم القیامة»(۸۵)

84) Aal-i-Imran, 18. 85) Al-Qiyamah, 5 - 6.

Messages

1- The manifestations of Allah's power and wisdom are endless. «سنریهم» (meaning, in addition to the signs that are accessible, We will show them signs in the future as well.)

2- The advancement of sciences is a step toward the knowledge of Allah. «سنریهم آیاتنا فى الآفاق و فى انفسهم»

3- The entire existence is a classroom for the knowledge of Allah. «فى الآفاق و فى انفسهم»

4- The knowledge of Allah is possible by paying attention to the closest being (man himself) up to the farthest point of existence. «فى الآفاق و فى انفسهم»

5- Allah completes the proof (Hujjah) for everyone. «سنریهم آیاتنا... حتّى یتبیّن لهم انّه الحقّ»

6- The knowledge of Allah must be accompanied by realization and belief. «یتبیّن لهم انّه الحقّ»

7- He is the only true object of worship. «انّه الحقّ»

8- Awaken dormant natures (fitrahs) through questioning. «اولم یكف...»

9- The Holy Essence of Allah is a Witness and proof unto Himself. «انّه على كلّ شى ء شهید», just as the friends of Allah (Awliya) used to say: «یا مَن دلّ بذاته على ذاته» (O He Who guides to His Essence by His Essence). (The sun is its own proof.)

10- If faith in Allah becomes certain, then faith in the Resurrection (Ma'ad), which is a manifestation of Allah's power, wisdom, and justice, is established. «الا انّهم فى مریة...»
